Topic (Instruction Focus):
Guest Instructor: Jared Henderson — Introduction to C and Computing (prog01)

 Block 1: Jared's background and career path; why C matters; compilation vs interpretation (gcc pipeline); binary, hex, and overflow; ASCII and character encoding.

 Block 2: Types and memory (int, float, char, sizeof); pointers and memory layout (stack vs heap, malloc/free); strings as byte arrays; functions with typed signatures; scanf for user input.
Daily Objectives (Learner Will Be Able T: 1. Explain why C is foundational to modern computing. 2. Describe the compilation pipeline (preprocess, compile, assemble, link). 3. Convert between decimal, binary, and hexadecimal. 4. Identify ASCII values for common characters. 5. Declare typed variables (int, float, char) and use sizeof. 6. Explain pointers, memory addresses, and stack vs heap.
State Competencies (Standards): 2.3.1 ASCII character encoding; 2.3.2 Binary, hexadecimal, and overflow; 5.1.7 Compilers and compilation; 5.2.1 Data types and variables; 5.2.3 Arithmetic operations.
Assessment / Grade Entry: Formative: Follow-along code check — students should have working hello.c compiled with gcc by end of session.

Topic (Instruction Focus):
prog01 Session A — Syntax, Compilation, and Number Systems (prog01a_Task)

 Exercise 1: Translate JS/Python snippets to C (variable declaration, printing, if/else, loops, functions). Compile and run each.

 Exercise 2: Encoding and number systems — print ASCII values, uppercase conversion via arithmetic, binary printing, integer overflow exploration.
Daily Objectives (Learner Will Be Able T: 1. Translate basic programs from JS/Python to equivalent C code. 2. Compile C programs using gcc and fix compiler errors. 3. Print ASCII values of characters and perform character arithmetic. 4. Print a number in binary using loops. 5. Demonstrate integer overflow behavior with unsigned char.
State Competencies (Standards): 5.1.7 Compilers and compilation; 5.2.1 Data types and variables; 2.3.1 ASCII character encoding; 2.3.2 Binary, hexadecimal, and overflow; 5.4.6 Testing and debugging; 5.4.7 Error identification.
Assessment / Grade Entry:
Due: prog01a_Task (Session A exercises)


Formative: Compiled and working C programs for each exercise

Topic (Instruction Focus):
prog01 Session B — Functions, Arrays, and Memory (prog01b_Task)

 Exercise 3: Functions, types, and math — temperature converter, clamp function, weighted average, grade calculator.

 Exercise 4: Arrays and loops — find max, compute average, reverse array in place.

 Exercise 5: Strings and dynamic memory — my_strlen, Caesar cipher, malloc/scanf/free exercise.

 Certification Prep — Student-driven certification study (CodeHS, Stukent, GMetrix, etc.). Select from: Data Analytics; HTML/CSS; JavaScript; AI; Database; Device Configuration; MOS.
Daily Objectives (Learner Will Be Able T: 1. Write C functions with typed parameters and return values. 2. Distinguish between int and float division in calculations. 3. Use arrays with loops to find max, average, and reverse elements. 4. Implement string functions using pointer arithmetic and null terminators. 5. Allocate and free dynamic memory using malloc and free. 6. Work toward industry certification goals.
State Competencies (Standards): 5.2.1 Data types and variables; 5.2.3 Arithmetic operations; 5.3.8 Nested loops; 2.3.1 ASCII character encoding; 5.4.6 Testing and debugging; 5.4.7 Error identification.
Assessment / Grade Entry:
Due: prog01b_Task (Session B exercises)
